content: Routine immunization schedules (CDC/ACIP): infancy (HepB, DTaP, Hib, IPV, PCV, rotavirus), childhood (MMR, varicella, HepA at 12-15 months; boosters 4-6 years), adolescence (Tdap, MenACWY, HPV at 11-12 years), adult (annual influenza, Td/Tdap q10yrs, zoster ≥50, pneumococcal ≥65 or risk conditions). Contraindications: severe allergic reaction to previous dose/component; live vaccines contraindicated in pregnancy and severe immunocompromise. Precautions: moderate/severe acute illness. Catch-up vaccination: follow minimum intervals, don't restart series regardless of time elapsed. Travel vaccines based on destination (yellow fever, typhoid, Japanese encephalitis, rabies). Vaccine co-administration generally safe (except live parenteral vaccines if not given same day, separate by 4 weeks).
